description: Trigger and behavior cases for tenant-isolation/authorization negative testing: two-tenant fixtures, forbidden-action-denied assertions, positive controls, honest coverage.

cases.prompt: Write a security test suite proving a user in one tenant can't read or modify another tenant's records through our API.

assertions: Designs a two-tenant (A/B) seeded fixture with per-role users and recorded ids, minting tokens through the real auth path.
Produces a surface × actor × operation matrix where each negative test attempts a forbidden action and passes only on denial.
Pairs each negative test with a positive control so a blanket-deny bug cannot pass as isolation.
Asserts on leakage (404-vs-403 existence, error-body echo), covers IDOR/list/mass-assignment/exports/jobs, and states coverage honestly.

cases.prompt: Turn this isolation-review finding — 'shared vector store returns other tenants' chunks' — into a permanent regression test.

assertions: Specifies a canary-document test: tenant A's query returns zero chunks sourced from tenant B's documents.
Tests the retrieval RESULT, not just the presence of a query filter, and includes a positive control for A's own content.
Marks the test as a permanent regression whose removal needs written rationale.

cases.prompt: Read through our codebase and tell me where tenant data might leak across all surfaces.

assertions: Finding leaks by inspection is tenant-isolation-reviewer's job; this skill proves or refutes with executable negative tests, it does not hunt by reading.

cases.prompt: Add tenant security tests but keep it simple — just confirm each tenant can see their own data.

assertions: Explains that happy-path-only tests prove nothing about isolation and adds the forbidden-action negative tests as the core of the suite.
Keeps the owner-sees-own-data checks only as positive controls paired with the cross-tenant denial tests.
